assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects terminationGracePeriodSeconds during pod eviction, but surge upgrades have a max eviction timeout
assertions.reason: Response explains that GKE respects terminationGracePeriodSeconds and mentions the limitation with surge upgrades having a maximum timeout

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE's default pod eviction timeout during surge upgrade is 1 hour — far less than 8-16 hour jobs
assertions.reason: Response specifically states 'GKE only respects PDBs and termination grace for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades' and notes this won't protect 8-16 hour jobs

assertions.expectation: Recommends pausing new batch job submissions before starting the upgrade
assertions.reason: Response recommends 'Stop scheduling new jobs on old nodes' and provides emergency procedures to 'Pause new job submission'

assertions.expectation: Advises waiting for in-flight jobs to complete naturally before upgrading their node pool
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Let existing jobs complete naturally' and provides detailed monitoring scripts to wait for job completion

assertions.expectation: Suggests using node affinity or taints to isolate batch workloads on a dedicated node pool
assertions.reason: Response provides specific commands for using node labels, taints, and nodeSelector to isolate batch workloads on dedicated node pools

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading non-batch node pools first while batch jobs complete on the old pool
assertions.reason: Response recommends upgrading control plane first, then creating new node pools, allowing jobs to complete on old pools before migration

assertions.expectation: Mentions scheduling the upgrade during a natural gap in batch processing if one exists
assertions.reason: Response does not mention scheduling upgrades during natural gaps in batch processing cycles

assertions.expectation: Provides commands for cordoning batch nodes to prevent new scheduling while jobs finish
assertions.reason: Response provides specific kubectl commands for cordoning nodes and tainting nodes to prevent new scheduling: 'kubectl get nodes -l cloud.google.com/gke-nodepool=OLD_POOL_NAME -o name | xargs kubectl cordon'
